name: sla-monitoring
description: Disputes over Service Level Agreement violations: uptime, latency, response time, availability

rules.name: SLA Uptime Breach
rules.ruling_template:
SLA uptime breach confirmed. Actual uptime of {actual_uptime}% fell below the required {required_uptime}% threshold.
Monitoring data verified from submitted evidence.
Service credit is owed to the claimant.
rules.description: Service uptime fell below the agreed SLA threshold during the monitoring period

rules.name: SLA Latency Violation
rules.ruling_template:
SLA latency breach confirmed. Actual latency of {actual_latency}ms exceeded the maximum allowed {max_latency}ms.
Monitoring data verified from submitted evidence.
Service credit is owed to the claimant.
rules.description: Response latency exceeded the maximum agreed threshold

rules.name: Partial Service Degradation
rules.condition: actual_uptime < required_uptime AND actual_uptime >= degraded_threshold AND monitoring_period_confirmed == true
rules.ruling_template:
Partial service degradation detected. Actual uptime of {actual_uptime}% was below the {required_uptime}% SLA but above the {degraded_threshold}% degraded threshold.
Partial service credit recommended based on severity of degradation.
rules.description: Service experienced partial degradation but did not fully breach SLA

rules.name: Incidents Within Acceptable SLA
rules.ruling_template:
Incidents were recorded but uptime remained within acceptable SLA bounds.
Actual uptime of {actual_uptime}% met or exceeded the required {required_uptime}% threshold.
No service credit is owed.
rules.description: Recorded incidents occurred but uptime remained within agreed SLA bounds

rules.name: Insufficient Monitoring Data
rules.ruling_template:
Insufficient monitoring data to determine if SLA was breached.
No uptime, latency, or availability evidence was provided from monitoring systems.
Case requires additional monitoring evidence for resolution.
rules.description: Monitoring data is insufficient to determine if SLA was breached
